note: Conformance vectors for libteleproto3 v0.2.0. XFAIL-PENDING-1.7 status lifted. Epic 1a (W-003): T3_CMD_BENCH=0x04 added. Story 7-1: A-003/A-005/A-006/A-007/A-009 vectors added; kdf-cross-csprng section added. Story 11-6: padding-frame, flag-negotiation, ctr-continuity-padding sections added (W-004, lib-v0.2.0). Bumped to schema v2 in Story 11-6-A to support optional payload_len. Story 12-7: secret-transport-mode vectors activated (A-012); http-stream-framing section added (W-006, Epic 12).

secret-format.note: Producer MUST reject; consumer accepts and routes `?q=1` suffix into result.query (A-010 split rule). Host stays DNS-clean. Host TLD .invalid per RFC 2606 §2.
Producer MUST reject; consumer accepts and routes `#frag` suffix into result.fragment (A-010 split rule). Host stays DNS-clean. Host TLD .invalid per RFC 2606 §2.
Producer MUST reject; consumer accepts and routes `?q=1` suffix into result.query (A-010 split rule).
Producer MUST reject; consumer accepts and routes `#frag` suffix into result.fragment (A-010 split rule).

Boundary: exactly 512-byte host with no path; A-005 ceiling PASS pure-host form (sister of a005-domain-len-512-with-trailing-slash)
Octet-vs-codepoint: 256 × U+00B5 (each 2 octets in UTF-8) = 512 octets PASS. Locks octet-counter semantics; a codepoint-counter parser would mis-PASS at 257 × U+00B5 (514 octets)
Octet-vs-codepoint reject: 257 × U+00B5 = 514 octets > 512 ceiling. A codepoint-counter parser would mis-PASS at 257 codepoints
Minimum-size leading-slash: domain = single 0x2f octet, total_len = 18 (passes rule 1 len≥2), rule 4 fires
Path-position control byte: rule 6 covers buf[17..len-1] including path region; offset 28 (post-slash)
Path-position NUL byte: rule 6 fires on 0x00 in path region

kdf-cross-csprng.note: KDF output MUST be byte-identical regardless of CSPRNG backend. Expected bytes pending Linux reference-impl run.
t3_init() MUST return T3_ERR_INVALID_CONFIG (lib detail-code) for unrecognised CSPRNG backend value; wire class is INVALID_ARG per spec/secret-format.md §5 closed set

padding-frame.note: 0xFE as first decrypted byte -> padding frame detected
0x00 as first decrypted byte -> not a padding frame
0xFF as first decrypted byte -> not a padding frame
0xFD as first decrypted byte -> not a padding frame
0x01 (MTProto payload start) -> not a padding frame
1-byte payload [0xFE] — minimum padding frame (marker only). payload_len=1 distinguishes this from pad-detect-0xfe which tests detection regardless of length.
multi-byte payload starting with 0xFE is a padding frame

flag-negotiation.note: flags=0 is valid at all versions, padding not supported
flags=0x0001 (T3_FLAG_PADDING) is MALFORMED at v0.1.x parser
flags=0x0001 (T3_FLAG_PADDING) is valid at v0.2.0 parser
flags=0xFFFF is MALFORMED at all versions (undefined flags)
flags=0x0002 (reserved bit 1) is MALFORMED at all versions
flags=0x0003 (bit0=T3_FLAG_PADDING + bit1=reserved) is MALFORMED at v0.2.0 — undefined bits MUST be rejected even if T3_FLAG_PADDING is also set

ctr-continuity-padding.note: AES-CTR advances by 16 (A), then 8 (padding), then 16 (B). Final position 40.
AES-CTR advances by 32 (A), then 4 (padding 1), then 12 (padding 2), then 32 (B). Final position 80.

secret-transport-mode.note: ?t= with empty value (§2.4 rule 4): empty value string defaults to T3_TRANSPORT_WS=0.
?T=1 — uppercase 'T' key. §2.4 rule 2: the key 't' is case-sensitive; 'T=1' does not match the prefix 't=' and defaults to T3_TRANSPORT_WS=0.
?t=-1 — negative value is not a valid base-10 unsigned integer (§2.4 rule 2); treated as unknown, defaults to T3_TRANSPORT_WS=0.

domain_len=512 (A-005 ceiling): 508 bytes 'a' (host-only, no '/') + 4 bytes '?t=1' = 512. The '?' appears in the host region; A-012 §1.1 applies: host truncated to 508 'a' chars, query='t=1', transport_mode=1. Verifies the A-005 ceiling is enforced on the raw domain including the ?t=1 suffix.

http-stream-framing.description:
Encode a single byte (0x41='A') into an HTTP chunk: header '1
' + data + trailer '
' = 6 bytes total.
http-stream-framing.note: 1-byte payload: hex len='1' (0x31), CRLF, 0x41, CRLF. Total 6 bytes.

http-stream-framing.description:
Encode 16 bytes into an HTTP chunk: header '10
' + 16 bytes + trailer '
' = 22 bytes total.
http-stream-framing.note: 16-byte payload: hex len='10' (0x31 0x30), CRLF, 16 bytes, CRLF. Total 22 bytes.

http-stream-framing.description:
Encode 255 bytes into an HTTP chunk: header 'ff
' + 255 bytes + trailer '
' = 261 bytes total.
http-stream-framing.note: 255-byte payload: hex len='ff' (0x66 0x66), CRLF, 255 bytes (0x00..0xFE), CRLF. Total 261 bytes.

http-stream-framing.description:
Encode 4096 bytes into an HTTP chunk: header '1000
' + 4096 bytes + trailer '
' = 4104 bytes total.
http-stream-framing.note: 4096-byte payload: hex len='1000' (0x31 0x30 0x30 0x30), CRLF, 4096 bytes of 0xAA, CRLF. Total 4104 bytes. data_fill=repeat means repeat data_hex byte for data_len bytes.

http-stream-framing.description:
Terminal (last) chunk: '0

' = 5 bytes. Signals end of chunked body.
http-stream-framing.note: Terminal chunk: '0' (0x30) CRLF CRLF = 5 bytes. RFC 9112 §7.1.

http-stream-framing.description:
Parse a well-formed 1-byte chunk '1
A
': data=0x41, data_len=1, consumed=6.
http-stream-framing.note:
Parse roundtrip of chunk-encode-1byte output: '1
A
'.

Terminal chunk '0

': size=0 signals end of chunked body. data_len=0, consumed=5.

http-stream-framing.description: Parse truncated chunk header '1' (no CRLF): must return MALFORMED.
http-stream-framing.note: Truncated chunk header: only size digit '1', no CRLF. Buffer too small for a complete header.

http-stream-framing.description:
Parse chunk header 'xz
' — non-hex size digits: must return MALFORMED.
http-stream-framing.note:
'xz
' — 'x' and 'z' are not valid hex digits. Parser must reject with MALFORMED.

http-stream-framing.description:
Parse '3
AB' — size says 3 bytes but only 2 data bytes available: must return BUF_TOO_SMALL.
http-stream-framing.note:
Chunk header says 3 bytes ('3
') but only 2 data bytes 'AB' present. Parser must signal BUF_TOO_SMALL.
